edges.description: HbS polymer formation deforms erythrocytes and drives sickling.
Rigid sickled erythrocytes increase microvascular obstruction and endothelial interactions.
Repeated membrane injury promotes erythrocyte destruction and clearance.
Microvascular obstruction causes ischemic vaso-occlusive pain episodes.
Recurrent vaso-occlusion causes ischemia-reperfusion injury in end organs.
Nitric oxide depletion and oxidative stress drive progressive endothelial and tissue injury.
Sustained erythrocyte destruction contributes to chronic hemolytic anemia.
Lower hemoglobin concentration reflects anemia from ongoing hemolysis and marrow compensation limits.
Elevated reticulocytes reflect marrow response to red-cell destruction in chronic hemolysis.
Higher indirect bilirubin reflects heme catabolism from accelerated red-cell breakdown.
Higher LDH reflects intravascular hemolysis and has been associated with hemolysis-linked vasculopathy risk.
HbS fraction identifies the polymerizing hemoglobin substrate; risk depends on genotype, oxygenation, concentration, and modifying hemoglobins.
Higher HbF is associated with reduced HbS polymerization and can serve as a pharmacodynamic readout for HbF-inducing therapies.

evidence.reference_title: A Phase 3 Randomized Trial of Voxelotor in Sickle Cell Disease.
evidence.snippet: At week 24, the 1500-mg voxelotor group had significantly greater reductions from baseline in the indirect bilirubin level and percentage of reticulocytes than the placebo group.

readouts.interpretation: Higher indirect bilirubin reflects heme catabolism from accelerated red-cell breakdown.

nodes.description: Intravascular hemolysis releases free hemoglobin, scavenging nitric oxide and causing endothelial dysfunction, pulmonary hypertension, and stroke risk.

evidence.reference_title: Dysregulated arginine metabolism, hemolysis-associated pulmonary hypertension, and mortality in sickle cell disease.
evidence.snippet: hemolysis contributes to reduced nitric oxide bioavailability and endothelial dysfunction via release of erythrocyte arginase, which limits arginine bioavailability, and release of erythrocyte hemoglobin, which scavenges nitric oxide.

evidence.reference_title: Dysregulated arginine metabolism, hemolysis-associated pulmonary hypertension, and mortality in sickle cell disease.
evidence.snippet: The ratios of arginine to ornithine and arginine to ornithine plus citrulline are independently associated with pulmonary hypertension and increased mortality in patients with sickle cell disease.

evidence.reference_title: Dysregulated arginine metabolism, hemolysis-associated pulmonary hypertension, and mortality in sickle cell disease.
evidence.snippet: hemolysis contributes to reduced nitric oxide bioavailability and endothelial dysfunction via release of erythrocyte arginase

nodes.description: Repeated vaso-occlusion and ischemia-reperfusion injury cause progressive damage to spleen, kidneys, lungs, and brain.

evidence.reference_title: DNA polymorphisms at the BCL11A, HBS1L-MYB, and beta-globin loci associate with fetal hemoglobin levels and pain crises in sickle cell disease.
evidence.snippet: Interindividual variation in fetal hemoglobin (HbF) expression is a known and potentially heritable modifier of SCD severity. High HbF levels are correlated with reduced morbidity and mortality.

evidence.reference_title: Effect of hydroxyurea on the frequency of painful crises in sickle cell anemia. Investigators of the Multicenter Study of Hydroxyurea in Sickle Cell Anemia.
evidence.snippet: In a previous open-label study of hydroxyurea therapy, the synthesis of fetal hemoglobin increased in most patients with sickle cell anemia, with only mild myelotoxicity.

readouts.interpretation: Higher HbF is associated with reduced HbS polymerization and can serve as a pharmacodynamic readout for HbF-inducing therapies.

evidence.reference_title: A Phase 3 Randomized Trial of Voxelotor in Sickle Cell Disease.
evidence.snippet: Deoxygenated sickle hemoglobin (HbS) polymerization drives the pathophysiology of sickle cell disease. Therefore, direct inhibition of HbS polymerization has potential to favorably modify disease outcomes. Voxelotor is an HbS polymerization inhibitor.

readouts.interpretation: HbS fraction identifies the polymerizing hemoglobin substrate; risk depends on genotype, oxygenation, concentration, and modifying hemoglobins.

evidence.reference_title: A Phase 3 Randomized Trial of Voxelotor in Sickle Cell Disease.
evidence.snippet: The primary end point was the percentage of participants who had a hemoglobin response, which was defined as an increase of more than 1.0 g per deciliter from baseline at week 24 in the intention-to-treat analysis.

evidence.reference_title: Dysregulated arginine metabolism, hemolysis-associated pulmonary hypertension, and mortality in sickle cell disease.
evidence.snippet: hemolysis contributes to reduced nitric oxide bioavailability and endothelial dysfunction via release of erythrocyte arginase, which limits arginine bioavailability, and release of erythrocyte hemoglobin, which scavenges nitric oxide.

readouts.interpretation: Lower hemoglobin concentration reflects anemia from ongoing hemolysis and marrow compensation limits.

nodes.description: Deoxygenated HbS polymerizes into rigid fibers, deforming red blood cells into sickle shape. HbS has glutamic acid to valine substitution at position 6 of beta-globin.

evidence.reference_title: Sickle cell dehydration: Pathophysiology and therapeutic applications.
evidence.snippet: Due to the unique dependence of Hb S polymerization on cellular Hb S concentration, cell dehydration promotes polymerization and sickling.

evidence.reference_title: Hemoglobin S polymerization: primary determinant of the hemolytic and clinical severity of the sickling syndromes.
evidence.snippet: Our results support the hypothesis that the intracellular polymerization of HbS is the primary determinant of the severity of both anemia and clinical symptomatology in the sickle hemoglobinopathies.

evidence.reference_title: Lactate dehydrogenase as a biomarker of hemolysis-associated nitric oxide resistance, priapism, leg ulceration, pulmonary hypertension, and death in patients with sickle cell disease.
evidence.snippet: We hypothesized that serum LDH may represent a convenient biomarker of intravascular hemolysis and NO bioavailability, characterizing a clinical subphenotype of hemolysis-associated vasculopathy.

readouts.interpretation: Higher LDH reflects intravascular hemolysis and has been associated with hemolysis-linked vasculopathy risk.

evidence.reference_title: Heme triggers TLR4 signaling leading to endothelial cell activation and vaso-occlusion in murine sickle cell disease.
evidence.snippet: Heme rapidly (5 minutes) mobilized Weibel-Palade body (WPB) P-selectin and VWF onto EC and vessel wall surfaces and activated EC nuclear factor κB (NF-κB).

nodes.description: Sickled RBCs are rigid and fragile, leading to hemolytic anemia and shortened RBC lifespan (10-20 days vs normal 120 days).

evidence.reference_title: Sickle cell dehydration: Pathophysiology and therapeutic applications.
evidence.snippet: In double heterozygosis for Hb S and C (SC disease) dehydration is the determining factor in disease pathophysiology.

evidence.reference_title: Lactate dehydrogenase as a biomarker of hemolysis-associated nitric oxide resistance, priapism, leg ulceration, pulmonary hypertension, and death in patients with sickle cell disease.
evidence.snippet: In a cohort of 213 patients with sickle cell disease, we found statistically significant associations of steady-state LDH with low levels of hemoglobin and haptoglobin and high levels of reticulocytes, bilirubin, plasma hemoglobin, aspartate aminotransferase, arginase, and soluble adhesion molecules.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated reticulocytes reflect marrow response to red-cell destruction in chronic hemolysis.

nodes.description: Sickled cells adhere to vascular endothelium and obstruct microcirculation, causing tissue ischemia and pain crises. Inflammation amplifies adhesion.

biological_processes: leukocyte adhesion to vascular endothelial cell

evidence.reference_title: Heme triggers TLR4 signaling leading to endothelial cell activation and vaso-occlusion in murine sickle cell disease.
evidence.snippet: intravascular hemolysis in SCD releases heme that activates endothelial TLR4 signaling leading to WPB degranulation, NF-κB activation, and vaso-occlusion.
